author: @esk8jaimes
text: Glad to hear you had such a fun experience with this, just wait until your try electric skateboards!

author: @stevetobias4890
text: Forgot to turn on image stabilisation Luke. What camera did you use while you were out and about?

author: @matheusdi3788
text: @Luke Neame would you get me the model (brand) of this scooter?

replies.author: @MyNameIsLukey
replies.text: Hi mate the brand of this specific scooter in the video is "Bird Scooters" I hope this helps! :)

replies.author: @matheusdi3788
replies.text: @MyNameIsLukey my friend you are a genius, thanks :D

author: @BoB4jjjjs
text: I can see lots of problems with them though, stolen, breaking down, no charge, going flat, complaints about putting a £! to start them finding it has no power then having to start another.  Will someone turn off that bloody bells!!!!

author: @PVflying
text: What stops people nicking them?  Even if they don’t function without the app, there must be parts (motor, batteries, speed controller, bms, etc..) which could be stripped off them?

replies.author: @greggrimer1428
replies.text: Nothing.  They can be nicked and have the tracker removed and a new controller fitted for 20 quid.  You'd need to buy a charger though

author: @cimota72
text:
There's a problem with the implementation of these. Independent ownership is still illegal but we have opened up the public domain to large operators (like Bird). We're way behind the rest of the world on personal electric vehicles.

These should be linked to public transport and park'n'ride car parks. Anything to reduce the number of cars entering a town or city. And yeah, we need more segregated cycle/light vehicle lanes....

author: @bobbod8069
text: The law around e-scooters, e-bikes, e-cargo carriers etc, is a little outdated and needs a revamp I think.

author: @lightcapmath2777
text: Very very cool video..keep them up DVD:)

replies.author: @lightcapmath2777
replies.text: @MyNameIsLukey Still have that coin slot collector but it is for US coins. your if you can use it. peace DVD:)

author: @greggrimer1428
text: They will be nicked and hacked.  It is easy to rip out the GPS tracker and rig the controller.  Paint it another colour

author: @robertbarker7709
text: Please get back to doing the real stuff soon.

author: @danielsheldrake
text: When you use a private e-scooter you can fold them up and bring them into the office therefore not needing a dedicated parking space.

replies.author: @MyNameIsLukey
replies.text: That is true! I think it might be illegal to use them unless you're on private land though. The laws do need a revamp in my opinion. :)

author: @stevvieb
text: We have them in Milton Keynes for a while. They are a real blight always in the way left by the side of the road or on paths in the way of buggy's. Not to talk about the people nearly knocking you over as they weave in and out of pedestrians. The seem like a good idea at first but the type of people using them are the type of people that shouldn't be let loose on them. You're supposed to be 18+ with a provisional license but so many younger teenagers on them, there must be a loophole in the app.

author: @edwardhughes352
text: They have these in basildon essex as well. Different make though.

replies.author: @MyNameIsLukey
replies.text: That is awesome! It seems like they are popping up all over the country. :)

author: @NorthernKitty
text:
Where I live, they need to be snowmobiles for most of the year, LOL!!!  🥶❄️☃️❄️🥶
(In fact, it's snowing as I type, LOL!!)
Great camerwork @ 2:40, that was a really neat perspective to watch it go.  😊

replies.author: @MyNameIsLukey
replies.text:
LOL that would be awesome! We had some snow here at the start of the year, it was amazing but super cold, I only came off my bike a million times as well LOL.
It's a shame I did not have my phone recording in landscape though, but watching it zip past the camera is quite funny! Thanks! :)

author: @mikecane
text: These eScooters are just about worldwide. Big market for FWG's paper batteries to replace the 18650s they use.

author: @jorgealonso2238
text: Hey Luke, they are very popular here in downtown Toronto, and an efficient fun way to see the city even if your 50+. Give it a try fellas, it makes you grin.

replies.author: @MyNameIsLukey
replies.text: That is awesome! I find that there is so much to see and not enough time, having one of these would help with that a lot. It a cool bonus that they are really fun lol. Cheers mate! :)

author: @synchro-dentally1965
text: In the US there are people called "juicers" that go around collecting the e-scooters to recharge them.

replies.author: @MyNameIsLukey
replies.text: "Juicers" I like that lol. I was wondering how they were charged. Thanks mate! :)

author: @dav1dbone
text:
Enjoyable video Luke,  looks like an interesting idea for getting around, also love hearing the bells - something we miss up in the "hustle and bustle" of the suburbs of Scotland;-)
How do they get charged? You'd think whoever set this scheme up would have installed wireless charging hubs etc, at the designated points, what's to stop a thief taking them and bypassing the lock out circuitry?

author: @keithking1985
text: they must be using coils under the path at the charge points. I wonder if you can charge your phone at one of these points???

replies.author: @mikecane
replies.text: No. Either the eScooter is collected to charge (via AC) or they have a removable battery that is swapped out.

author: @whatnextjoe
text: I think a service like this is great to have available if you could use it , if I had no use for it I'd be happy for my friends that might use it. Somebody put in a lot of time and cash flow to make this available for the public it could save you in pinch. Thanks for sharing, Thank you Luke

replies.author: @MyNameIsLukey
replies.text: I agree, the friends I have talked to said it has saved them from being late to things many a time. I think that is super cool. Thanks mate! :)

author: @তুহিন_জানা
text: we had a bicycle service like this in Kolkata. Everyone loved it. They had to close cuz bikes kept breaking :(

replies.author: @MyNameIsLukey
replies.text: I can imagine the average wear and tear after being used every day can really take its toll. It's sad they had to shut it down though, because that sounds super fun!

author: @Mrbullydog66
text: These are in Essex too.  Local police are confiscating non approved rental ones.   Looks like a money spinner.  Monopoly on transport.

author: @hackjealousy
text: The real question is how you held the potato while you were filming with it on the scooter.

replies.author: @MyNameIsLukey
replies.text: Hahahaha with great difficulty mate, I don't know why it came out like that considering it was shot on an iPhone 8 plus.

author: @johnwinters1705
text: It seems like a great idea for City use but in rural areas it just doesn't seem practical.

author: @herbetone
text: I think although they may be fun to use, but the general public needs more exercise not less, do not take this comment personally Luke.
